While the main brewpub's dining area closed in 2025, the site continues to serve as a production facility and hosts the upstairs Abbey Bar, a premier venue for live music and special events in Central Pennsylvania.

attractions.tips: Shows at The Abbey Bar are generally 21+ unless accompanied by an adult over 25. It is highly recommended to check the online events calendar and purchase tickets in advance for popular shows. Free, well-lit parking is available in two large lots behind the building.

It features an exhibit hall displaying the workings of freshwater wetlands, an indoor bird viewing area, a children's discovery area, and a nature shop, all designed to foster environmental education and appreciation for local wildlife. (Official Website, Dauphin County)

attractions.name: Boyd Big Tree Preserve Conservation Area
attractions.description: The 1,025-acre Boyd Big Tree Preserve Conservation Area straddles Blue Mountain and serves as a habitat for numerous large tree species and deep forest birds, particularly warblers. It features an extensive 12-mile trail system for hiking and cross-country skiing, a spring-fed pond, and a large wildflower meadow that attracts butterflies and field birds during late summer. PA DCNR, Visit Hershey & Harrisburg

It offers a nostalgic "Americana" experience with professional baseball, miniature golf, riverboat tours, and historic rides. The island serves as a central hub for family fun and provides panoramic views of the Harrisburg city skyline (Visit Hershey Harrisburg, Visit Pennsylvania).

attractions.description: Fort Hunter Mansion and Park is a 40-acre historic site situated on a bluff overlooking the Susquehanna River and Blue Mountain. Originally a frontier fort during the French and Indian War, the site evolved into a prosperous farm and private estate, and now serves as a public museum and recreational park featuring an elegantly restored 19th-century Federal-style mansion. Official Website, Visit Hershey & Harrisburg

attractions.name: Gettysburg National Military Park
attractions.description: Gettysburg National Military Park preserves the site of the 1863 Battle of Gettysburg, a pivotal Union victory and the bloodiest battle of the American Civil War. The park features over 1,300 monuments, the iconic Gettysburg National Cemetery, and the site of President Abraham Lincoln's famous Gettysburg Address. It serves as a profound memorial to the soldiers who fought there and a gateway to understanding a turning point in United States history. (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: Park Grounds & Roads: Daily, 30 minutes before sunrise to 30 minutes after sunset. Museum & Visitor Center (March–November): Mon-Sun 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM; (December–February): Mon-Sun 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM. (National Park Service, Gettysburg Foundation)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year's Day; early closure at 1:00 PM on Christmas Eve). (Gettysburg Foundation)
attractions.admission: Park Grounds: Free. Museum, Film & Cyclorama Experience: Adults (13-64) $20.75, Seniors (65+) $19.75, Veterans $19.75, Youth (6-12) $15.75, Children (5 and under) Free, Active Duty Military Free. (Gettysburg Foundation)

attractions.tips: Reserve timed tickets for the Film, Cyclorama, and Museum experience at least 15 minutes in advance. Dress for variable weather and extensive walking, and be sure to bring water, sunscreen, and insect repellent during summer visits. attractions.description: Harrisburg Cemetery, also known as Mount Kalmia Cemetery, is a prominent 19th-century rural cemetery and national historic district in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania. Founded in 1845, it features a park-like setting designed with picturesque landscape philosophies and serves as an arboretum with over 600 trees. The grounds contain the remains of numerous governors, politicians, and veterans from the Revolutionary War and Civil War. Wikipedia, Official Website

attractions.name: Historic Ephrata Cloister
attractions.description: Founded in 1732 by Conrad Beissel, the Historic Ephrata Cloister was a unique communal religious society of celibate Brothers and Sisters and married Householders. Today, this National Historic Landmark preserves one of the most significant collections of 18th-century Germanic-style architecture in the United States. Visitors can explore the community's rich legacy of original a cappella music, intricate Fraktur art, and early American printing history. Official History, Discover Lancaster
attractions.hours: Wednesday – Saturday: 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 4:00 PM; Monday – Tuesday: Closed. Official Website
attractions.days_open: Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
attractions.admission: Adults (ages 12-64): $10.00, Senior Citizens (65+) & AAA Members: $9.00, Youth (ages 3-11): $6.00, Children (under 3): Free. Active duty military and their families are also admitted free. Official Website, PA Bucket List
highlights: ["The Saron (Sisters' House) and Saal (Meetinghouse), two of the largest 18th-century Germanic-style buildings in America.", 'A significant legacy of original a cappella music and German calligraphic art known as Fraktur.', "The site of an ambitious printing establishment that produced 'Martyrs Mirror,' the largest book printed in colonial America.", 'Its role as a hospital for nearly 250 American soldiers during the Revolutionary War.', 'Interactive visitor experiences including an orientation video and the Museum Store.']
attractions.tips: Guided tours are the only way to see the interior of the main historic buildings and typically last about 1 hour and 15 minutes. Plan to spend at least 1.5 to 2 hours total to view the introductory video and explore the grounds. Photography is permitted on the grounds for personal use, but flash and tripods are not allowed inside buildings. Official Visit Page, Lonely Planet

attractions.name: John Harris-Simon Cameron Mansion
attractions.description: Built around 1766 by John Harris Jr., the founder of Harrisburg, this National Historic Landmark is one of the oldest stone houses in the region. It was later purchased in 1863 by Simon Cameron, Abraham Lincoln's first Secretary of War, who transformed it into a grand Victorian-style mansion. Today, it serves as the headquarters for the Historical Society of Dauphin County and displays a vast collection of period furnishings and artifacts. (Historical Society of Dauphin County)

attractions.name: Walnut Street Bridge (The People's Bridge)
attractions.description: The Walnut Street Bridge, affectionately known as 'The People’s Bridge,' is a historic wrought iron truss bridge originally built in 1890. It serves as a popular pedestrian and bicycle link between downtown Harrisburg's Riverfront Park and City Island, offering panoramic views of the Susquehanna River. The bridge was closed to motor vehicles following Hurricane Agnes in 1972 and remains the oldest surviving bridge on the river.

attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Access may vary for specific City Island events)
attractions.days_open: Daily
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: ['Oldest surviving bridge connecting downtown Harrisburg to City Island, built in 1890.', 'Recognized as a Historic Civil Engineering Landmark and listed on the National Register of Historic Places.', "Features a unique 'Phoenix column' truss design by the Phoenix Bridge Company.", 'Closed to motor vehicles, providing a safe and scenic 0.5-mile path for pedestrians and cyclists.', 'Outlined with lights at night, creating a beautiful visual landmark over the Susquehanna River.', "Western spans collapsed in 1996 due to ice floes, making it a unique 'bridge to nowhere' on the western side."]
attractions.tips: Wear practical shoes for the open-grate walkway and be careful with small items like phones that could fall through. Visit at sunset or nighttime to see the bridge beautifully illuminated. Parking is available on City Island or in downtown garages near Front Street.

attractions.name: Echo Dell – Indian Echo Caverns
attractions.description: Indian Echo Caverns is a premier natural underground attraction featuring stunning limestone formations and crystal-clear lakes. Visitors can enjoy a 45-minute guided tour through the caverns, which maintain a constant temperature of 52 degrees year-round. The site also includes outdoor activities like a petting zoo, gem mining, and a playground. Echo Dell
attractions.hours: Memorial Day to Labor Day: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM daily. Labor Day to Memorial Day: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM daily (Closed Tuesdays and Wednesdays Jan 1 - Mar 10). Echo Dell
attractions.days_open: Daily (Open 7 days a week; closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, and New Year’s Day). Echo Dell
attractions.admission: Adults (ages 12+): $24.50, Seniors (ages 65+): $22.00, Children (ages 2-11): $14.50, Children (under 2): FREE. Discounts available for AAA and military. Echo Dell
highlights: '45-minute guided interpretive tour of natural limestone caverns [Echo Dell', 'Crystal Lake, a stunning six-foot-deep underground pool Visit Hershey & Harrisburg', 'Discovery Barnyard Petting Zoo with goats, alpacas, and more Echo Dell', 'Gem Mill Junction for seasonal gemstone and fossil panning Echo Dell', 'Spacious gift shop featuring authentic Indian items and souvenirs Echo Dell']
attractions.tips: The cavern is a constant 52 degrees, so a light jacket is recommended. Wear closed-toe shoes like sneakers or boots as paths can be wet. Strollers and wheelchairs are not permitted due to stairs and narrow passages. Pets are allowed but must be held for the duration of the tour. Echo Dell

attractions.name: Hersheypark

Hershey as leisure grounds for employees of Hershey's Chocolate Factory, Hersheypark is now a world-class family amusement park in Central Pennsylvania. The park features 15 thrilling roller coasters, a 11-acre water park, and a zoo, offering "Hersheypark Happy" experiences for guests of all ages. Hersheypark About Us

attractions.name: ZooAmerica North American Wildlife Park
attractions.description: ZooAmerica North American Wildlife Park is a year-round, 11-acre walk-through zoo home to more than 200 animals native to the continent of North America. The zoo is divided into five distinct regions: Southern Swamps, Great Southwest, Eastern Woodlands, Big Sky Country, and Northlands (ZooAmerica).
attractions.hours: Daily 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Winter/Spring); Summer hours (late May through August) 10:00 AM - 7:00 PM (ZooAmerica).
attractions.days_open: Open year-round, except Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day (ZooAmerica).
attractions.admission: Regular (Ages 9-61): $16.50; Junior (Ages 3-8): $14.50; Seniors (Ages 62+): $14.50; Children (2 & Younger): FREE; ZooAmerica Pass Holders: FREE; Hersheypark Season Pass Holders: FREE. All tickets subject to amusement tax (ZooAmerica).
highlights: 'Home to more than 200 animals from five regions of North America ([ZooAmerica)', '11-acre walk-through wildlife park with native plants and landscaping (ZooAmerica)', 'Behind-the-Scenes Tours and animal feeding encounters available (ZooAmerica)', 'AZA-accredited institution committed to wildlife conservation (ZooAmerica)', 'Educational programs and seasonal events like Creatures Of The Night (ZooAmerica)']
attractions.tips: The zoo is cashless, so bring a credit or debit card. Allow approximately 1 to 1.5 hours for a complete walk-through. Dress for the weather as the entire experience is outdoors (ZooAmerica FAQs). Admission is free if entered through the Hersheypark bridge during park hours (ZooAmerica).

attractions.name: Appalachian Brewing Company
attractions.description: Appalachian Brewing Company is one of the largest microbreweries in the United States, specializing in handcrafted ales, lagers, and artisanal craft sodas. While its original Harrisburg brewpub closed in 2025, the company continues to operate its flagship 26,000-square-foot Mechanicsburg location, which features a full-service restaurant, an innovative brewing system, and an interactive bottling facility. Visit PA, Appalachian Brewing Company

attractions.name: Dickinson College
attractions.description: Founded in 1773, Dickinson College is a private liberal arts college in Carlisle, Pennsylvania, and was the first college chartered in the newly independent United States. The institution is recognized for its 144-acre historic campus featuring limestone-clad buildings and a strong commitment to global education and environmental sustainability. Visitors can explore the National Historic Landmark Old West building or visit the college's dedicated art museum, the Trout Gallery. Dickinson College, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Yellow Breeches Creek (also known as Callapatscink Creek)
attractions.description: Yellow Breeches Creek is a world-renowned limestone stream and designated Pennsylvania Scenic River that meanders approximately 49 to 56 miles through Cumberland Valley's farmland, forested areas, and suburbs. It is a premier destination for fly-fishing, kayaking, and tubing, eventually joining the Susquehanna River near New Cumberland.
attractions.address: 534 Park Drive, Boiling Springs, PA 17007 (Main Access Point)
attractions.hours: Daylight hours (Sunrise to Sunset)
attractions.days_open: Daily (7 days a week)
attractions.admission: Free (All tiers)
highlights: ['Designated as a Pennsylvania Scenic River, recognized for its natural beauty and ecological significance.', 'Famous for its "Catch and Release" fly-fishing section near Boiling Springs, attracting anglers from across the country.', 'A popular 49-mile Water Trail with multiple access points for kayaking, canoeing, and tubing adventures.', 'Home to rare species such as the Bog Turtle and Eastern Mud Salamander within its diverse watershed.', 'Flows through the historic Messiah University campus and beautiful parks like Lower Allen Community Park.', 'Boasts a mix of calm pools, gentle riffles, and scenic limestone landscapes throughout its course.']
attractions.tips: Always check real-time water levels via the USGS Camp Hill gauge (ideal levels are 1.4-2.0). Visitors should bring their own equipment as rentals are limited, and it is recommended to travel with at least two vehicles to facilitate shuttling between entry and exit points.
